programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: A plane takes off during a snowstorm and soon reports both pilots are dead. As the passengers slowly die off, they wish they had never left the ground.

programme.title: The Shed
programme.desc: A teen and his best friend endure nonstop torment from bullies at school, but that soon changes when one discovers a bloodthirsty creature that resides in a country shed.

programme.title: Cabin Fever: Patient Zero
programme.desc: Vacationers think they've found the perfect place for a bachelor party, but a deadly, flesh-eating virus is waiting to infect them.

programme.title: The Vigil
programme.desc: Terror strikes when an Orthodox Jewish man agrees to watch over the dead body of a community member in a dilapidated Brooklyn home.

programme.title: Moloch
programme.desc: When she and her family are attacked by a random stranger one night, a Dutch woman sets out to find an explanation. The more she digs, the more she becomes convinced that she is being hunted by something ancient.

programme.title: Exam
programme.desc: Eight candidates cooperate in order to secure a job with a prestigious company.

programme.title: Impetigore
programme.desc: A woman inherits a house in her ancestral village, but she is unaware that members of the community have been trying to locate and kill her to remove the curse that has plagued them for years.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.title: Stranded
programme.desc: Astronauts (Christian Slater, Brendan Fehr) come under attack by dangerous alien spores after a meteor collides with their moon base.

programme.title: Come True
programme.desc: Strange events plague a troubled teen when she participates in a scientific experiment to stop her nightmares.

programme.title: Hellbender
programme.desc: A teen and her mother live simply in a home in the woods, spending their time making metal music. A chance encounter with a fellow teen causes her to uncover a connection between her family and witchcraft, which causes a rift with her mother.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: Ride
programme.desc: Struggling actor James pays his bills by driving for a ride-sharing service in Los Angeles. His night soon takes a shocking turn when Bruno, armed with a gun, takes James and a woman on a terrifying, white-knuckle trip that spirals out of control.

programme.title: Z
programme.desc: A boy's imaginary friend terrorizes his family.

programme.title: Revenge
programme.desc: A shocking act leaves a woman brutalized and left for dead by three sleazy men. Unfortunately for her assailants, she survives and soon begins a relentless quest for bloody revenge.

programme.title: God's Country
programme.desc: A college professor gets drawn into an escalating battle of wills after she catches two hunters trespassing on her property in the remote mountains of the American West.

programme.title: Some Kind of Hate
programme.desc: Once a victim of vicious bullying, a teenage girl (Sierra McCormick) rises from the grave to punish the tormentors of a new student (Ronen Rubinstein) at a remote school.

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: An outsider seeks bloody vengeance against the sadistic cult leader who destroyed his peaceful existence.

programme.title: Re: Born
programme.desc: A legendary soldier with a mysterious past decides to unleash his inner beast to stand up for what he cares about.

programme.title: Mayhem
programme.desc: Fired from his law firm, a man and a former client must fight for their lives when a mysterious virus causes employees to become violent and aggressive. They join forces to reach the executives on the top floor and settle the score once and for all.

programme.title: The Owners
programme.desc: An elderly couple turn the tables on a group of young thieves who broke into their house while they were away.

programme.title: Contracted
programme.desc: A young woman (Najarra Townsend) begins bleeding, shedding maggots and hallucinating after a stranger date rapes her at a party.

programme.title: David Attenborough: A Life on Air
programme.desc: Michael Palin presents a profile of the television career of David Attenborough, from controller of BBC Two to his wildlife programmes such as Life on Earth and The Blue Planet.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: The first ever episode of the landmark natural history series Life on Earth. David Attenborough explores the wildlife and landscape that inspired Charles Darwin's theory of evolution.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following a pair of tigers released in a nature reserve. They must fight for territory and learn to hunt. Will they mate and start a family, or will they be killed?

programme.title: The Magic of Dance
programme.desc: Dance has almost always moved freely across frontiers. Just as in the 19th century, Italian and French dancers went to Russia to make their names, so in the 20th century, Diaghilev reversed the flow by bringing the Ballets Russes to live and work in Western Europe. All part of the ebb and flow of dance. Margot Fonteyn visits Tchaikovsky's house near Moscow and talks to Marie Rambert about Diaghilev. The programme includes unique film of Pavlova, as well as Mikhal Baryshnikov dancing Petrushka.

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: An Omnibus special, first broadcast in 1995, shortly after the death of Ginger Rogers. The film looks at how the legendary Fred and Ginger musicals were made and features anecdotes from Rogers and clips from Astaire's last television interview.

programme.title: Puttin' on the Ritz: The Genius of Fred Astaire
programme.desc: A short compilation of clips from Fred Astaire's days at the RKO studio, where he formed an unforgettable partnership with Ginger Rogers.

programme.title: Dance Passion Swansea
programme.desc: Dancers of all kinds take over Swansea’s streets, landmarks, parks and beaches to showcase the city, explore what home means and celebrate the life-enhancing joy of dance. The performances feature solos, duets and ensembles across a variety of dance styles, including ballet, Latin, contemporary, ballroom, breaking, traditional clog and Welsh folk. Locations range from Mumbles Pier and skateboard park to Swansea Bay and the dunes, beaches and bays of the Gower peninsula.

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.desc: Explore astonishing wild places and the most spectacular moments from Planet Earth III.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: The first ever episode of the landmark natural history series Life on Earth. David Attenborough explores the wildlife and landscape that inspired Charles Darwin's theory of evolution.

programme.title: David Attenborough: A Life on Air
programme.desc: Michael Palin presents a profile of the television career of David Attenborough, from controller of BBC Two to his wildlife programmes such as Life on Earth and The Blue Planet.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The first round of the quiz competition for students continues. Cranfield University makes its first appearance since 1999 against four students from Royal Holloway, University of London. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. It is all about making connections between things which may appear, at first glance, not to be connected at all.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.desc: It was one of the greatest vanishing acts in history. More than 3,000 years ago a mysterious and ruthless civilisation rose from nothing, created a brutal and unstoppable army and built an empire that rivalled Egypt and Babylon. Yet, just as it was at the height of its powers, the great empire suddenly vanished from history. This is the story of the formidable Hittites, a civilisation bent on world domination. Their long-lost capital, Hattusha, which disappeared thousands of years ago, was recently rediscovered. Archaeologists have unearthed one of the most astonishing and ingenious cities of the ancient world, featuring rings of impenetrable walls, secret tunnels, temples, palaces and a vast pyramid-like structure facing Egypt. Buried in this lost city is one of the greatest libraries of the ancient world. All the secrets of the mysterious Hittite empire were written in two codes - one a unique form of hieroglyphs. Using these deciphered texts, the film recreates the ancient world of the Hittites, telling the story of what happened to them, and what caused an empire built to last forever to vanish so completely from history.

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Scandinavia - a land of extremes, on the edge of Europe. Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the extraordinary art to come out of the dark Norwegian soul, most famous for producing The Scream by Edvard Munch.

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team explore the remarkable story of Jodrell Bank Observatory and its towering Lovell Telescope, which scientists have used to listen to the cosmos for almost 70 years.

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.desc: In the years preceding 1914, David Bomberg, Walter Sickert and Paul Nash set out to paint a new world, but, as the century unfolded, found themselves working in the rubble. David Bomberg is now recognised as the most startlingly original British painter of his generation, but died in obscurity more than half a century ago. A Jewish immigrant from London's east end, his early modernist works pushed art to its limits. Fighting at the Somme, David Bomberg watched the world splinter and fall apart just like the works of art he had created. Bomberg spent the rest of his life searching for order in an increasingly disordered world, and his wanderings took him as far as Palestine, before he settled at the end of his life in Ronda, Spain. When he died in 1957, embattled and in poverty, he seemed to be no more than a footnote in the history of British art. However, the works that survive David Bomberg tell their own story. Combative and iconoclastic, he remains the most elusively original British painter of the 20th century.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: With the industrial revolution transforming the British Isles, the cities and country are divided, and artists respond to the upheavals to lives and the landscape.

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: In the first of a series of city adventures, Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke head to Lisbon, rapidly becoming one of Europe’s most popular tourist destinations. Winding through the city’s cobbled streets, from its steep hills to the picturesque shore line, the cultural riches they encounter reveal the city's fascinating history. From a spectacular monument to the maritime globetrotting of Portugal’s golden age and the work of a photographer documenting the city's large African population, they discover a complex history of former glories and a darker, slave-trading past. Their journey also uncovers the impact of 20th-century dictatorship on the city's artistic and cultural life, through the work of contemporary artists Paula Rego and Joana Vasconcelos. And they discover how the city's location on the west coast of Europe, looking out to the Atlantic, has shaped the cosmopolitan spirit of the city. In one of the city's Fado clubs, Alastair and Nina enjoy the popular Portuguese folk music, whose beautiful melodies celebrate a yearning for home, once sung by sailors dreaming of their return.

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: An Omnibus special, first broadcast in 1995, shortly after the death of Ginger Rogers. The film looks at how the legendary Fred and Ginger musicals were made and features anecdotes from Rogers and clips from Astaire's last television interview.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: With the industrial revolution transforming the British Isles, the cities and country are divided, and artists respond to the upheavals to lives and the landscape.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The University of Glasgow takes on Queen's University Belfast in another first-round match in the quiz competition for students. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. It is all about making connections between things which may appear, at first glance, not to be connected at all.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is dreading being invited to Onslow's birthday party almost as much as he dreads her agreeing to come. However, she changes her mind when she learns that Rose's new boyfriend, a well-to-do Greek businessman, will be picking her and Richard up from their home in his limousine. In true Hyacinth fashion, she decides to hold a cocktails and canapes party starting an hour before the limousine's planned arrival time so that the invited crowd will see the prestigious form of transport being used. Sadly, things do not go quite as she expected...

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Mother is out to get Aunt Esme's money for Timothy, and Timothy has encounters with a garage owner and his dogs.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama's epic history reaches the 18th century and the birth of modern Britain. Due to an economic explosion, the consumer society is born, agriculture becomes big business and London becomes the fastest growing city in Europe. However, many in Scotland are unhappy with the union of the Scottish and English parliaments. When Bonnie Prince Charlie and his Jacobite army advance on London, the country's new-found peace and prosperity are threatened.

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: Fabio Capello leads Milan to a new era of glory, while Berlusconi steps onto Italy’s political stage, ushering in a turbulent new chapter in the nation’s politics.

programme.title: Florence Nightingale: Nursing Pioneer
programme.desc: This film follows the life of an extraordinary woman who revolutionised modern nursing and whose legacy continues to benefit millions. Born into a rich English family, Florence Nightingale broke through social and gender barriers to pioneer a profession previously reserved for men. Dedicating her life to helping those in need, she was a trailblazer who led a group of nurses to care for wounded soldiers during the Crimean War and developed revolutionary views about hygiene and sanitation. Hailed as a heroine by Queen Victoria and the British people upon her return from the front, Florence went on to establish the Nightingale Training School for Nurses and reform healthcare at home and abroad. Florence Nightingale died in 1910 aged 90, leaving behind an inspirational legacy.

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team explore the remarkable story of Jodrell Bank Observatory and its towering Lovell Telescope, which scientists have used to listen to the cosmos for almost 70 years.

programme.title: Dam Busters Declassified
programme.desc: Martin Shaw takes a fresh look at one of the most famous war stories of them all. The actor, himself a pilot, takes to the skies to retrace the route of the 1943 raid by 617 Squadron which used bouncing bombs to destroy German dams. He sheds new light on the story as he separates the fact from the myth behind this tale of courage and ingenuity. Using the 1955 movie The Dam Busters as a vehicle to deconstruct the raid, he tries to piece together a picture of perhaps the most daring attack in the history of aviation warfare. Along the way, Shaw hears from the last RAF veteran of the raid, as well as a German survivor of the tsunami which resulted from the Moehne dam's destruction.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: Talking Movies takes a look at some classic French cinema. France boasts one of the most successful and thriving film industries in Europe. Tom Brook and some fellow cineastes pick their favourite French films and tell the stories behind their creation, from 'Breathless', the movie that heralded the French New Wave, to 'Amélie', the film that shows Paris to perfection. These are all French films that broke new ground and as we hear from their fans, they feel as vibrant today as when they were first made.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson, in discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, analyses the Iranian position in their confrontation with the United States in the Gulf, examines the state of play in Russia's war with Ukraine and looks at the chances of Benjamin Netanyahu surviving as Israeli prime minister after elections later this year.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Twenty years ago a man arriving for a job interview at BBC Television Centre was taken into a live TV studio and asked to comment on a legal story. The clip quickly spread on early video-sharing sites and became a meme. Guy Goma reflects on what happened, alongside Elliott Gotkine, the journalist involved in the original error. Saudi Arabia has spent large sums on sport and media in recent years, aiming to build influence as well as make financial returns. New developments, including changes to sports funding and involvement in major media deals, suggest a shift in approach. Claire Enders, founder of Enders Analysis, and Claire Atkinson, contributing editor at The Ankler and founder of Media Mix, outline how the strategy is changing. Climate change remains one of the most complex stories for news organisations to cover. It involves long-term change, scientific detail and questions about how to keep audiences engaged. David Shukman, journalist and former BBC Science Editor, sets out his view on how climate reporting has developed and the issues it faces.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Climate Question
programme.desc: The oceans cover about 70 per cent of the planet and are crucial for life on Earth. Oceanographer Helen Czerski reveals how the seas around us regulate the climate and the weather, even in the desert.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Clive Myrie’s Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive heads for the east coast of Barbados to check out the world’s oldest rum distillery. He explores the sugar cane fields and the factory floor before he gets the chance to have a sip of the golden produce. He puts a shift in with the hens on an organic family farm that stretches out for over 100 acres, tests out his flexibility alongside British expats with some cliff-top yoga and finally meets a young Bajan spoken word poet at the Builders of Barbados wall.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg talks to Bridget Phillipson MP, education secretary; Catherine West MP, Labour; James Cleverly MP, shadow housing, communities and local government secretary; Richard Tice MP, deputy leader of Reform UK; and Rhun ap Iowerth MS, Plaid Cymru leader. On the panel are Jeremy Vince, Nadine Dorries and Sharon Graham.

programme.title: The President's Path
programme.desc: Sumi Somaskanda, Bernd Debusmann and Fin Gomez talk about the ongoing war in Iran and the upcoming midterm elections. Trump's presidential approval rating has been steadily declining in recent months, with polls hitting some of his lowest numbers of the term this week. With concerns over the ongoing war in Iran and rising costs at home will the president hit the campaign trail to boost support, and will it be enough?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Twenty years ago a man arriving for a job interview at BBC Television Centre was taken into a live TV studio and asked to comment on a legal story. The clip quickly spread on early video-sharing sites and became a meme. Guy Goma reflects on what happened, alongside Elliott Gotkine, the journalist involved in the original error. Saudi Arabia has spent large sums on sport and media in recent years, aiming to build influence as well as make financial returns. New developments, including changes to sports funding and involvement in major media deals, suggest a shift in approach. Claire Enders, founder of Enders Analysis, and Claire Atkinson, contributing editor at The Ankler and founder of Media Mix, outline how the strategy is changing. Climate change remains one of the most complex stories for news organisations to cover. It involves long-term change, scientific detail and questions about how to keep audiences engaged. David Shukman, journalist and former BBC Science Editor, sets out his view on how climate reporting has developed and the issues it faces.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Climate Question
programme.desc: The oceans cover about 70 per cent of the planet and are crucial for life on Earth. Oceanographer Helen Czerski reveals how the seas around us regulate the climate and the weather, even in the desert.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: What Happened at...?
programme.desc: The 1986 explosion at Chernobyl triggered a public health emergency across Europe. Today, the site's strategic location continues to be a flashpoint in Russia's war with Ukraine. Forty years on from the disaster, Jordan Dunbar travels to Chernobyl to examine the events that led to the world's worst nuclear accident and what we can learn from them today.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights from the day’s five Premier League games, including Manchester City against Brentford and Liverpool versus Chelsea. Elsewhere, Sunderland host Manchester United at the Stadium of Light, Fulham face Bournemouth at Craven Cottage, and Brighton take on Wolves.

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg talks to Bridget Phillipson MP, education secretary; Catherine West MP, Labour; James Cleverly MP, shadow housing, communities and local government secretary; Richard Tice MP, deputy leader of Reform UK; and Rhun ap Iowerth MS, Plaid Cymru leader. On the panel are Jeremy Vince, Nadine Dorries and Sharon Graham.

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news, interviews and debate.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A stunning multimillion-pound project in London that Tommy first saw in 2021 reaches an epic conclusion. Martel encounters two characterful projects in North Yorkshire and Cumbria.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Antiques experts help the reds and the blues race against the clock to uncover hidden gems. Will they make money in the auction room - and win the coveted Golden Gavel?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman’s buyers are already renting in the beautiful countryside of Herefordshire, but they need Nicki’s help to find them a house of their own that comes with enough land for a holiday let. Watch out for a surprise visit from another of the show's presenters, who also famously lives in the county.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits the historic city of Lincoln to explore stories of faith and to introduce modern worship music alongside hymns from the historic cathedral.

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC’s television shows. Email your thoughts to pov@bbc.co.uk

programme.title: Despicable Me 3
programme.desc: Having been fired from the Anti-Villain League, Gru is flabbergasted to discover that he has a twin brother who is eager to re-establish the family tradition of supervillany, but first they must tackle Balthazar Bratt who has stolen the biggest diamond in the world.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how a remote garden on Scotland’s west coast has become an oasis for barn owls and pine martens as they battle the elements to feed, breed and survive.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Wild London
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough explores the wildlife of his London hometown, from urban deer to rooftop peregrines and many others, revealing how nature thrives in the world’s greenest major city.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Adam Henson visit Oswestry Livestock Market, one of the region’s largest weekly cattle and sheep markets. As spring trading peaks, they step into a fast-moving world where fortunes can change in minutes. With livestock numbers falling and markets closing nationwide, Oswestry remains a crucial lifeline, not just for trade, but for farming communities to come together.

programme.title: BAFTA Television Awards
programme.desc: The prestigious BAFTA Television Awards return with host Greg Davies at the helm. It has been a landmark year for storytelling, with Adolescence, A Thousand Blows and Amandaland all vying for top honours. From groundbreaking drama to the best in entertainment and factual programming, the creativity that defined the last twelve months will be celebrated. With special performances from internationally acclaimed Norwegian artist AURORA and multi-platinum singer-songwriter, and star of The Celebrity Traitors, Cat Burns. Viewers will be treated to a night of glamour, heartfelt speeches and spectacular live music as the best TV of 2026 is crowned.

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty flails as Ning continues to push him. Meanwhile, Leanne and Gary bond, until he pressures Leanne to betray Matty to safeguard her family.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Kelly Cates presents highlights from the day’s four Premier League games including West Ham against Arsenal, a game that could have huge implications at the bottom of the table and in the title race. With European places at stake, Aston Villa travel to relegated Burnley, and Everton are at Crystal Palace, while Nottingham Forest look to move clear of the relegation zone at home to Newcastle.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team revive a cherished rocking horse, a spectacular set of conga drums, and a logbook that played a key role in British LGBTQ+ history.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate how a firm providing equipment to children with special educational needs has let down dozens of families and charged unlawful fees. Also on the programme, the retiree who sent her second-hand car back to the dealership for repairs but instead received a call from police saying it had been found vandalised. And the advice clinic heads to Reading to help a woman who received a parking ticket while on holiday, despite leaving her vehicle in the care of an airport parking firm.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Whether it’s a bungalow in Stoke, a mixed commercial and residential unit in Nottinghamshire or an end-of-terrace in north Wales, damp is the order of the day in all of today’s properties!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Railway Wood is a replica trench in a Kent woodland based on a First World War trench in Belgium. David Harper walks the duckboards with a historian who helped to create it.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Rav and Nick listen in to a highly skilled scammer, posing as a broadband provider, who claims to fix issues that you might have with your internet connection. This slick scammer takes full control of a man’s phone and computer. With the victim completely under his influence, and the ground interceptors over an hour away, the team must try everything they can to disrupt the scam and reach the man before he pays out. Interceptor Mark also takes a deep-dive investigation into the world of sextortion scammers – fraudsters who blackmail their victims by threatening to release intimate pictures and videos.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps ex-England international and football hard man Neil ‘Razor’ Ruddock find his softer side as he and his wife Leah escape to the country in rural Kent. Razor’s tough-guy persona is put to the test when a 15th-century thatched cottage causes him concern about spiders, but the former pro shows that he is still as sharp as ever in a house hunt full of wit and warmth.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Bristol-based Diana and her daughter Millie are dreaming of a fresh start in the sunny Somerset countryside, with enough land for a little flower farm to bloom. Alistair Appleton is on hand to help them sow the seeds of their new life. Along the way, he meets a couple who have swapped cider apples for grape vines, planting their very own vineyard in the heart of this amazing county.

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: In this episode, the experts lend their skills to a community project, hear the story behind a new treasure to be repaired and visit a local crafting hero. York Minster is one of our most magnificent cathedrals, boasting the world’s largest expanse of medieval stained glass. But the stonework of this glorious gothic monument is crumbling away on a daily basis. Will and Dom join the new generation of stonemasons being trained to save it, harnessing modern technology while still using centuries-old skills. Will’s Yorkshire odyssey continues in Cawthorne, where Elwin presents him with a huge drum that has been owned by the local marching band since the 1850s. The damaged paintwork, ill-fitting skins and loose cords aren’t befitting such a magnificent instrument, so percussion expert Pete Woods takes on the mammoth task of getting it back into a playable condition. Dom heads further south through God’s Own Country to visit occasional Repair Shop expert Jonathan Reid in his Sheffield workshop. Here the master putter, or scissor-maker, is keeping the endangered craft of making scissors by hand alive, and he’s eager to share some secrets of the trade with his fellow craftsman.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where four contestants – in this episode, basketball team Terry, Charlie, Meg and Kathy – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian makes a worrying discovery, Nicola takes matters into her own hands, and Grant helps a friend.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It’s the last round of heats. A new batch of amateur cooks enter the MasterChef kitchen ready to show off their culinary skills, hoping to get through to the next round.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon and Dylan finally spend time together. He wants to mend their relationship, but she’s not ready to listen – until a journey into his past helps her understand. Meanwhile, a different side of Ollie comes to light as she encounters an old foe. Cat’s insecurities get the better of her, and she doesn’t know where to turn – until she and Ollie finally find common ground... and a bar.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon is ready to take control of her life, even if it means building dangerous new alliances. Determined to protect her family, she turns her attention to Sam. As chaos builds, she, Cat and Ollie become increasingly desperate, until Shannon makes a bold decision. Can the family trust each other to keep themselves safe?

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts, with guest panellists comedian Jon Richardson and actress Sheila Hancock joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: What's Love Got to Do with It?
programme.desc: In London, an award-winning film-maker documents her best friend's journey into an assisted marriage in line with his family's Pakistani heritage. In the process, she challenges her own attitude towards relationships.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The team hear from the devastated homeowners who claim the warehouses behind their back gardens were built without proper consultation. Also on the programme, the ‘postcode lottery’ of accessible taxis – and the plight of one couple who feel trapped in their home, as they say the number of wheelchair accessible vehicles has dwindled in their area. And at the advice clinic in South Shields, expert help for the bereaved woman whose parents’ headstone still hadn’t been installed a year after she paid for it.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two properties with plans for both to be turned into serviced accommodation in London and Kent. Dion’s in Stoke, and rather than dancing on the ceiling. he’s bouncing on the floors!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents a special show on a matter close to her heart – art! In Scotland’s capital city Edinburgh, she finds out about a group of famous local artists known as the Scottish colourists and speaks to the grandson of one of them. She also picks out some arty purchases from the show’s archives and counts down the most profitable items at auction. Along the way, Christina Trevanion visits the studio of an art restorer who brings works of art back to their former glory, and Charlie Ross is in mid Wales to meet an artist who sculpts countryside animals from clay.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: The team investigate the work of a scammer pretending to be from Amazon. He is trying to scam a man in the UK by saying that hackers have access to his online account, but it’s all a lie designed to get him to hand over money. The team’s access to the scammer is unprecedented, meaning they can see him running his criminal scam, live from his bedroom via his own webcam. And having access to the fraudster’s computer means that Nick and Harleen can learn almost everything about him – giving them the perfect opportunity to confront him.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone is hoping that the New Forest property market hits all the right notes as he enlists the help of Sonali Shah to find him and his family a new home in the Hampshire countryside. With mum Sian along for assistance, Gareth has one big request: to find a property with enough space to fit a grand piano! A beautifully converted former schoolhouse may just provide the answer, but the endless possibilities and potential at a 1920s thatched cottage really strike a chord with the music maestro.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in stunning south west Wales, helping Carol, along with her husband, return to the country where she grew up. With her son Greg supporting her on the house hunt, she’s dreaming of a home close to the sea. The property line-up includes a picture-perfect double-fronted house with roses around the door, and a mystery house boasting spectacular mountain views... it’s Wales at its most wonderful.

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: The team help commemorate the victims of the Lockerbie bombing, automata expert Michael tackles a clockwork train, and Kirsten gets hands-on with medieval slipware pottery.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, family members Jan, Mick, Shirl and Dave – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea finds herself in hot water, Mark tries to ask for help, and George and Nicola face questions from their family.

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: Rylan and Angela Scanlon are live from Vienna for the first semi-final of the 70th Eurovision Song Contest. Expect powerful vocals and spectacular staging.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 1993. Niall is conflicted when asked by his mother to lie in court about Ruben’s violence. Ruben seems to have changed for the better, so the decision weighs heavily on him.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high-stakes quiz, joined by a celebrity team featuring comedian and actor Charlie Brooks, Dr Amir Khan, presenter Sara Damergi and comedian Tim Vine as they take on the Bridge of Lies in support of their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Louise is distressed to learn she's carrying a girl, not a boy. After Donna fires Tim, she is forced to work with depressed Janet and mad Munch. Gaz struggles to train Munch to be his best man, while Janet picks a theme for Donna and Gaz's wedding: 'dead Jonny'.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz works through his list of three things he must do before he dies. Donna gets work in a charity shop to try to overcome her fear of death. Louise stumbles across a massive secret about Corinthian.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: When the bailiffs come to repossess her house, Janet has just 24 hours to get some money or lose her home. Louise finds a rich criminal who seems keen to help but all he offers Janet is a spit roast. Gaz struggles to babysit his son Corinthian. Donna desperately awaits the call to an interview for her dream job.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: When Donna goes to London to get her dream job, Janet and Gaz are left alone together. After a night of Lambrini and candles they wake up and realise they've had sex again. Donna learns the art of bluff during her interview. Louise starts having contractions.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda has spent most of her life avoiding being set up by her mother Penny. Old school pal Tilly sets her up on a blind date with an army doctor friend, Dreamboat Charlie. The date doesn't go quite to plan and so Penny insists on throwing a party to introduce Miranda to a suitable man. Miranda is notoriously bad at coming up with excuses, but when she sees a photo of her intended date he is so bad that the only option left is to lie and come out to her mother in an attempt to stop her from matchmaking.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: When a handsome man leaves his wallet in the shop, Miranda and Stevie think it could be a sign, especially when they discover he's called Robert Husband. They become highly competitive and try to find out who he'd prefer to take to dinner if he had to choose one of them. Meanwhile, Tilly has invited Miranda to Henley Regatta under the strict proviso that she doesn't embarrass her. Miranda is determined to be able to cope at a tricky social occasion just once. Old friend Gary seems to be the only person who believes Miranda should just be herself. Gary, meanwhile, has been offered a new job in Hong Kong, but seems to be waiting for someone to persuade him to stay; will Miranda take the hint?

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola receives a shock, Phil and Sam support a loved one, and Ian desperately tries to gain extra votes.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola is forced to come clean to George, Ian makes a rash decision after being humiliated by Oscar, and Linda makes a decision for the sake of her family.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian instantly regrets his actions, Grant steps in to help a friend, and Gina and Harry are forced to come clean.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It’s election day, and Ian and Elaine are anxiously awaiting the results. Nicola tries to support George, and Max’s jealousy mounts.

During the First World War, two soldiers are ordered across no-man's-land and deep into enemy territory to deliver a message. If they fail, an entire battalion, under the mistaken belief the Germans are retreating, will be ordered over the top and into a deadly trap.

programme.title: James Arthur: Out of Our Minds
programme.desc: Multi-platinum-selling singer-songwriter James Arthur is at a crossroads in his life. After being propelled to stardom following his X Factor win in 2012, it has been a decade of highs but also desperate lows. James suffers from anxiety, depression and severe panic attacks. At his worst, he has considered taking his own life. Now 34, he is wondering if returning to the anti-depressants he has rejected might finally give him peace of mind. This documentary follows James as he explores the roots of his fragile mental health and searches for ways to cope.

programme.title: Roman Kemp: The Fight for Young Lives
programme.desc: On a daily basis, Roman Kemp is contacted by young people who are struggling. With people turning to him for advice, he’s questioning whether or not there is a worsening mental health crisis and asking if these young people should be offered more support in their own communities. This documentary follows Roman as he immerses himself in schools, youth centres and treatment settings around the country, meets with experts and charities, and visits the Houses of Parliament to try to understand the potential solutions for improving the mental health of young people in the UK today.

programme.title: Charlie Cooper’s Myth Country
programme.desc: Charlie Cooper, who wrote and starred in the BBC’s This Country, has a fascination for the weird and wonderful world of British folklore. In this episode, with the help of an old AA van, he goes in search of Black Shuck, the demon dog of East Anglia. Despite a local legend that seeing Shuck brings with it a curse, Charlie meets locals who claim they have survived such encounters. Keen to capture the beast on camera, he attempts an overnight stakeout, with unexpected consequences. Alleged sightings of this 900-year-old hell hound are recorded almost monthly, so is Charlie in with a chance? His humorous odyssey takes him across Norfolk and Suffolk, where he meets witnesses and learns that in Britain almost every county has its own mythical creature. You only need to scratch the surface anywhere in this land to discover that folklore, myth and legend are very much alive.

programme.title: Charlie Cooper’s Myth Country
programme.desc: Charlie Cooper’s odyssey takes him to Wiltshire, home to ancient sites such as Stonehenge, Avebury and the Westbury chalk horse. He wants to get to the bottom of why humans feel the need to leave marks on the landscape. He soon turns his investigation towards a far more recent phenomenon, crop circles, hoping to settle a debate that has plagued his family for years. They believe that crop circles are the work of aliens, but Charlie strongly doubts that. He meets with farmers and secretive 'croppies' (humans who create crop circles), trying to find out how these vast and intricate shapes appear overnight. Helped by his friend Scott, Charlie takes his research one step further and attempts to shed light on the phenomenon by making his very own crop circle. The humorous trip aboard an old AA van shows him that you only need to scratch the surface anywhere in this land to discover that folklore, myth and legend are very much alive and exert a beguiling power.

programme.title: Charlie Cooper’s Myth Country
programme.desc: Charlie Cooper has always loved the stories of King Arthur, especially the ones about his buried treasures. He digs out his metal detector, says goodbye to his family and embarks on an Arthurian investigation. He travels to Wales, the land of dragons and song, on a mission to discover if King Arthur was a real man or just a myth and if there’s any truth behind this treasure story. In the process, he meets treasure hunters of different sorts, a recorder of rare and ancient folk songs, and finds what he refers to as 'his tribe', a metal-detecting club from South Wales. He’s optimistic he will return home a rich man - if not in wealth, then certainly in knowledge.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: A Dalek fleet surrounds a lone rebel ship, and only the Doctor can help it now. With the Doctor facing his greatest enemy, he needs Clara by his side. Confronted with a decision that could change the Daleks forever, he is forced to examine his conscience and ask himself whether he is truly a good man.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: In a sun-dappled Sherwood Forest, the Doctor discovers an evil plan from beyond the stars and strikes up an unlikely alliance with Robin Hood. With all of Nottingham at stake, the Doctor must decide who is real and who is fake. Can impossible heroes actually exist?

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to the Mexican island of Cabo Pulmo, where the people have worked tirelessly to bring their surrounding oceans back to life and make it a marine reserve.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to meet some of the most endangered animals in southern Africa - wild dogs. He meets the people working to save them and explores why their survival is so important.

programme.title: EastEnders 2008 - 2012
programme.desc: The grieving of the Trueman/Fox family takes an unexpected turn, Phil makes a significant decision regarding Louise, and Darren follows the path of true love.

programme.title: EastEnders 2013 - 2017
programme.desc: The hunt continues for Lola and Lexi, and Phil is in a race against time to find them. Cora comes up with a plan to clear her name, and Ian and Denise spend the night together.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Christmas fever is in full swing in Walford as Sharon and Keanu prepare to say, ‘I do.’ The Mitchell family lunch is interrupted by a surprise visitor, while Jay receives an unexpected Christmas present. And in a flash, Denise, Kathy, Linda, Sharon, Stacey and Suki’s lives are permanently changed forever as a Walford male meets his fate in The Vic.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The police have news for Sonia, Denise tries to make sense of what’s happening, and Martin and Stacey take a trip down memory lane.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: An incident at Walford High sparks a heated debate.

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: The cast of the long-running soap explore the issues raised by Joel’s misogyny storyline. On the way, they meet individuals whose lives were turned upside down by the effects of misogyny and discover those who are trying to change the narrative.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. She goes to a high-octane pool party to discover how casinos are cashing in on the party scene.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir goes even further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting those who live and work there.

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Charlotte’s back and life is as crazy as ever. She's set herself a massive challenge for the business while being a mum to Alba and introducing Jake to the joys of Kama Sutra – all while cooking a fry-up!

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Charlotte pays an emotional tribute to Nana Jean by having a tattoo with her ashes. Despite getting a harsh reality check about the company finances from dad Gary, Charlotte sets her sites on the most extravagant Ibiza trip ever.

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Charlotte flies out a crew of 16 to Ibiza for a no-expenses-spared photo shoot; she’s rented a huge villa, chartered a yacht and has drafted her team of creatives and models to make this shoot look amazing. With Charlotte at the helm, the pressure is on - but will the distractions of the Ibizan night life throw the shoot off course?

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Jake struggles to organise a surprise party for Charlotte. Then, as the skies open, the summer fashion launch doesn’t go to plan. Is this the end of Charlotte’s business empire?

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: It's an emotional day in the Crosby household as Alba Jean starts nursery. Then, the numbers are in from the summer launch, and Charlotte finds out if they’ve sold enough to cover the Ibiza shoot. Jake’s shopping for a new luxury vehicle for his business and, like most new parents, Charlotte and Jake are struggling to squeeze in date nights - so they’ve decided to organise some blind dates. Let the competition begin!

programme.title: Charlotte in Sunderland
programme.desc: Charlotte is in Oz for a top-secret work project and has taken Jake and Alba with her. Letitia is awaiting the results of a mammogram and, to help keep Letitia distracted, Gary has booked them in for a pamper day at the place where they got married. Charlotte and Letitia chat weddings and try on Letitia’s wedding dress from 20 years ago!
