programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve goes in search of ocean acrobats, mischievous coyotes and deadlies in miniature - but he ends up having an encounter with a humpback whale he will never forget!

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall tracks cheetahs across Zambia by motorbike, witnessing epic hunting and the challenges faced by this endangered predator.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: With the leaderboard closer than ever, all four teams leave Zaamin National Park and race towards the next checkpoint, Almaty, Kazakhstan’s former Soviet-era capital.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: It’s the summer holidays, but teenager Pip is focused on her school research project investigating the case of 17-year-old Andie Bell, whose disappearance in 2019 has haunted the town of Little Kilton. Andie’s boyfriend Sal Singh sent a text confessing to the murder before being found dead, seemingly taking his own life. Andie’s body was never found. With her own secret connection to what happened five years ago, Pip is determined to prove Sal’s innocence.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Now a team, Pip and Ravi turn their attention to Andie’s best friends and find out Andie had a secret life that Sal was unhappy about. Meanwhile, Pip receives an anonymous warning.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip decides she must track down the drug dealer Max told her about, so against her better judgement, she seeks out a calamity party - an infamous secret teen party, full of drugs and sex. With the help of Cara and Lauren, Pip works out the next calamity party is the following weekend in the woods. Ravi is sceptical about Pip’s plan, but she’s determined to see it through and attend the party with Cara and Lauren.

programme.title: What's Love Got to Do with It?
programme.desc: In London, an award-winning film-maker documents her best friend's journey into an assisted marriage in line with his family's Pakistani heritage. In the process, she challenges her own attitude towards relationships.

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni explores the life of Sean 'Diddy' Combs, who has been jailed on prostitution-related charges She meets some of his inner circle to find out more about him.

programme.title: Margrete: Queen of the North
programme.desc: Powerful and tense historical epic about the untold story of the woman who united Scandinavia in a time of war. The year is 1402. Margrete has achieved what no man has managed before: she has gathered Denmark, Norway and Sweden into a peace-oriented union that she rules through her adopted son, King Erik. But a conspiracy is in the making, and Margrete finds herself in an impossible dilemma that could shatter her life’s work.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Reeling from a sudden loss, Shannon lashes out at her family. Only Ollie can offer her some comfort in the form of a dodgy vape. When Shannon’s day takes a surreal turn, she reconnects with herself and confronts her fears. But Dylan’s return snaps her back into a painful reality.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Dylan’s new life brings moments of comfort, but he also finds it challenging in a way he never saw coming. His old coping mechanisms no longer work, and he’s forced to ask himself what he truly wants. A conversation with Luke reminds him what’s at stake and what actually matters. Meanwhile, Cat seeks clarity from her son, hoping to understand the future she wants for herself and her family.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: This week, we’re in the Highlands and Islands of Scotland with nearly 5,000 km of coast. It’s home to five keen walkers, all determined to get their hands on the prize. Last to lead a walk is 32-year-old Rob from Dundee, who works for a communications company and plays amputee football for Partick Thistle. So what’s he got planned to win the golden walking stick? He’s leading the walkers to one of the Highland’s best-known spots. Glenfinnan Viaduct has been made famous the world over as the location that the Hogwarts Express steamed through in the Harry Potter movies. Will the infamous Scottish wet weather dampen the spirits and scotch Rob’s chances of winning?

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Vick Hope explore the chalk landscapes of the Chilterns, where rare grasslands, seasonal streams and specialised wildlife all depend on the fragile ground beneath their feet. But as habitats have been lost and broken into isolated fragments, some species have declined. Matt meets farmers working together to reconnect these landscapes, helps protect the endangered Duke of Burgundy butterfly and discovers how drone technology is transforming a chalkland vineyard. Vick visits a rare winterbourne stream that flows only after winter rains as restoration work helps bring it back to life. Meanwhile, Adam Henson welcomes a rare-breed middle white pig to his Cotswolds farm and trials crops that could cut fertiliser use.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie heads for Ben Ledi in the Trossachs to help with some high-altitude fieldwork. And 25 years on from the foot-and-mouth outbreak, Arlene finds out about a legacy that is helping today's farmers. Elsewhere, Rosie is in Gairloch Wester Ross to hunt for herring and find out how this over-exploited species is making a comeback. Leanna is in Fife, where she joins a team researching the movements of secretive snipe, and Cammy visits the Ayrshire couple determined to preserve and promote the Clydesdale horse breed.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Planting borders and planning for cut flowers are on Lizzie Schofield and Brian Cunningham’s to-do list at the garden. Lizzie is direct sowing in the new cuttings area and Brian is in the zig-zag border, planting varieties that will grow tall, making them ideal for the back of a plot without requiring support or staking. Brian kicks off this year’s collection of plants being cultivated in the 6x8ft glasshouse. This is the most common-sized domestic greenhouse, so if you are lucky enough to have one, Brian has some great ideas for this season. Lizzie is the first of the presenters to start work in her competition plot. The theme this year is planting that will appeal to one of the senses, and Lizzie has one of the most challenging: touch. Plus, there is a report on the alpine collection at the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh, and all the usual handy hints.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Big Zuu brings big flavours and big energy to the studio with his super malt ribs with a fiery jalapeno coleslaw and spiced suya potatoes. Sophie Wyburd whips up a brilliant mid-week Greek-style dinner with her pork and halloumi koftas, quick homemade flatbreads and an olive and cabbage salad. Tony Singh makes a delicious brunch, folding masala spices through scrambled eggs served with buttered asparagus and topped with port-glazed lobster. Helen McGinn has some top alternatives to wine, while Samin Nosrat shares her recipe for alphonso mango pie. Finally, Iain Stirling gets either his food heaven, halibut, or his food hell, prawns. Meanwhile, Rick Stein tucks into to some of the best Cumbrian produce, and Clive Myrie meets the Dominican Republic’s answer to Nigella Lawson.

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher brings together a selection of recipes from the nation's favourite television chefs. Each episode gives baking fans another chance to experience classic dishes from the likes of the James Martin, Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo. There's added insight and appreciation from Jane herself, too. Whether it's cakes, pies, puddings or main courses - this greatest hits of baking is a must for anyone with a need to knead, and should help inspire even more of us to start getting creative in the kitchen.

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini
programme.desc: Florence 1935. A circle of English ladies settled in the cultured city take an orphan boy, Luca, under their wing. But even a propaganda encounter with Il Duce cannot protect them when war comes, and it falls to Luca and two brash American women they barely tolerate to ease their existence. Semi-autobiographical drama from Franco Zeffirelli.

programme.title: Badminton Horse Trials
programme.desc: One of the most prestigious events in the world eventing calendar.

programme.title: Rugby League: Challenge Cup
programme.desc: The second semi-final sees a repeat of last year’s final as reigning champions Hull Kingston Rovers meet Warrington Wolves again.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary series. While badgers are easily recognized, remarkably few people have ever seen one alive. It's a creature blamed for destruction and disease, but surprisingly little is known about the sett society. We spy on them for over a year in a Devon valley, Big Brother style, with hidden underground cameras. Narrated by David Attenborough.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow travels to Berlin and Bletchley Park to mark the 80th anniversary of VE Day, featuring interviews with veterans and the stories of those who helped to liberate Europe.

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: In 1995, at the start of the Silicon Valley tech boom, Elon arrives with his younger brother Kimbal to join the gold rush and be part of the creation of the internet. They’re geeky young South Africans battling to make a fortune, but Elon is still sleeping on the floor of his office and washing at the local YMCA. Burdened with student debt, Elon and his brother create their first company, which they go on to sell for millions. Within just four years, at the age of 28, Musk is a multi-millionaire. But on his honeymoon, things start to go wrong.

programme.title: Scandinavia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first leg of Simon’s Scandinavian journey takes him from Svalbard, the beautiful archipelago that marks the northernmost outpost of Norway, south into the magical snowy region of Lapland. In freezing conditions, he travels to Nato's longest border with Russia, where tensions are high and even Santa's village is prepared for a potential war.

programme.title: The Iron Claw
programme.desc: Based on the true story of the inseparable Von Erich brothers, who made history in the intensely competitive world of professional wrestling in the early 1980s.

programme.title: Surge
programme.desc: Airport security worker Joseph lives a mundane existence with a daily commute and a repetitive job. After a frustrating reunion with his unresponsive parents, he cracks under the pressure and embarks on a frenzy of emotional release.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 1989. Niall is struggling at university when he invites Ruben to join him and his flatmates for freshers' week. What begins with excitement ends in devastating consequences.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts this quiz celebrating the UK and all its quirks. Alongside him, Traitors star Paul Gorton acts as an unreliable guide, leading a daily tour packed with fun, facts and fibs. This week’s celebrity contestants are TV icon Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and Radio 1Xtra DJ Remi Burgz. The week kicks off in Whitby, with a daily winner and scores building up to eventually reveal a weekly champion.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, Michael travels to South Korea’s south west, where food, protest and landscape reveal the values that have shaped the modern nation. Arriving by high-speed train in Gwangju, he explores the region’s deep-rooted food culture, meeting a prizewinning kimchi maker to learn how Korea’s iconic fermented dish has sustained communities for centuries and become a symbol of shared labour and tradition. Michael then confronts a darker chapter in South Korea’s history at the May 18th Memorial Park, where a survivor of the 1980 Gwangju Uprising recounts how student-led protests against military rule were brutally suppressed and why the events are now regarded as the cornerstone of Korean democracy. Leaving the mainland, Michael crosses by ferry to Jeju Island, a volcanic landscape dominated by Mount Halla. Exploring lava tubes with a volcanologist, he uncovers how the island was formed before meeting the Haenyeo, Jeju’s legendary female free divers.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how a remote garden on Scotland’s west coast has become an oasis for barn owls and pine martens as they battle the elements to feed, breed and survive.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Kelly Cates presents highlights from the day’s four Premier League games including West Ham against Arsenal, a game that could have huge implications at the bottom of the table and in the title race. With European places at stake, Aston Villa travel to relegated Burnley, and Everton are at Crystal Palace, while Nottingham Forest look to move clear of the relegation zone at home to Newcastle.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Celebrating the programme's 50th year, Clive Myrie presents the first heat of this series of the classic quiz where contenders face the famous black chair with the ultimate goal of becoming the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are Francisco de Goya, Peaky Blinders, Team GB at the 2020 Summer Olympics and the Philip Marlowe novels of Raymond Chandler.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions while our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The Clean It Fix It team is in West London to help the Parkinson family. With four young children, their house is overrun with toys, the hallway is a dumping ground, and the kitchen is in desperate need of a clean. The team has just one day to transform the space into a clean and clutter-free family home.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The annual flood has finally arrived, bringing new prey and new challenges for the big cats. Gordon Buchanan follows the hungry lion mothers as they hunt buffalo, whilst the eldest lion cub, Mathata, learns to avoid this formidable prey. Anna Dimitriadis tracks Mushi, a skinny young cheetah struggling to catch food in the changing landscape. Brad Bestelink is sure Xudum the leopard is pregnant and looking for a den, but rising flood waters are pushing dangerous male leopards into her territory.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A second visit to Royal Holloway in Surrey where Michael Aspel and the experts discover a belt clasp linking Annie Oakley and Queen Victoria, a ring with an unusual coloured diamond, two bargain jugs and a 'very rare' gold watch - not to mention the collection of dog collars, the box of wax doll's limbs and the Japanese rats.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The Flog It! team get frightened as Paul Martin heads to the spooky Dartmoor landscape, and Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey find strange things occurring in the queue. Paul Martin discovers a piece of music history with a platinum disc signed by the boys of Coldplay, and we talk to an expert in America about a walking stick associated with General Tom Thumb.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thrilling new railway adventure following the Silk Road through the most populous country of Central Asia, Uzbekistan. At the meeting point of east and west, this region has been home to some of the world’s greatest empires: ruled by the Mongols, the ferocious conqueror Tamerlane, imperial Russia and the Soviet Union. Michael begins in the capital, Tashkent, where he takes in its vast, vibrant market and samples popular Uzbek delicacies. He travels on the lavishly decorated Soviet-era metro, which was hidden from the outside world until 2018, when a ban on photographing it was lifted. Michael hears how an earthquake resulted in Tashkent becoming the capital of Soviet modernist architecture, and he makes his TikTok debut with a globally famous Uzbek social media star.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Andrew and dad Terrence are spinning plates on the farm, and with their glamping pods, just as the latest member of the Glenn family arrives. Long days on the dairy shed build and an infection in Adam’s leg mean he is desperate for a new prosthetic. Meanwhile, Ally and fellow crofters set sail to the stunning volcanic Shiant Isles, where they must navigate steep cliffs to find and shear sheep.

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: In 2009, the financial crash sends shockwaves across the world and threatens to cripple Elon. To realise his dream, Elon must gain the confidence of the financial markets to allow his electric car company Tesla to become viable. Elon’s space rocket enterprise, SpaceX, is awarded a Nasa contract and starts to enjoy success. However, Tesla is on the ropes and haemorrhaging money. To compound this pressure, Elon is about to marry his second wife, British actress Talulah Riley. Despite these challenges, Elon is determined to create a new car, the Model S, that he believes will be the first mass-produced all-electric car in history.

programme.title: Children of the Blitz
programme.desc: From September 1940 to May 1941, during the Second World War, over 35,000 tonnes of bombs and incendiaries were dropped on towns and cities across the UK during the infamous Luftwaffe aerial bombing campaign that became known as the Blitz. Just before the outbreak of war, the British government’s Operation Pied Piper put plans in place to evacuate millions of children, but only 800,000 left towns and cities that were vulnerable to attack. These are the stories of some of the two million children who stayed at home, often to help their families or because their parents couldn’t bear to send them away. This documentary features some of the last people who witnessed the reality of the Blitz, with their testimony offering an intimate, enlightening and moving portrait of wartime Britain through children’s eyes.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: A Bigger Splash
programme.desc: A convalescing rock star and her boyfriend holidaying on the Mediterranean island of Pantelleria see their private holiday disrupted when they receive an unexpected visit. In English and Italian with English subtitles.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the Usk valley, from Newport’s urban wildlife to ground-nesting birds, a huge lesser horseshoe bat roost and scarce firecrests.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Vick Hope explore the chalk landscapes of the Chilterns, where rare grasslands, seasonal streams and specialised wildlife all depend on the fragile ground beneath their feet. But as habitats have been lost and broken into isolated fragments, some species have declined. Matt meets farmers working together to reconnect these landscapes, helps protect the endangered Duke of Burgundy butterfly and discovers how drone technology is transforming a chalkland vineyard. Vick visits a rare winterbourne stream that flows only after winter rains as restoration work helps bring it back to life. Meanwhile, Adam Henson welcomes a rare-breed middle white pig to his Cotswolds farm and trials crops that could cut fertiliser use.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: With the leaderboard closer than ever, all four teams leave Zaamin National Park and race towards the next checkpoint, Almaty, Kazakhstan’s former Soviet-era capital.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: The oversight team face controversy around the tournament’s official new football when a conspiracy theorist claims it has suspect technology inside it. Will accidentally inspires a social media campaign for Sarah’s latest sustainability initiative. But as the ball issue escalates to cause political fallout, it looks as if Sarah’s campaign will be over before it starts. Someone needs to give a statement to the press. And it turns out that someone is Ian.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Hello playmates! Presenter Danny Sebastian admires a collection of items which once belonged to comedian and actor Arthur Askey. They include medals for school sports and his long service to entertainment, as well as wristwatches and theatre dressing room door plaques. It’s a double bill at Newark antiques fair as two couples try to make showstopping purchases. Each pair has £300 in their pockets and an hour on the clock. On the red team are civil servants Sarah and Graeme, and in blue are Helen and Nick. Both teams show an early interest in furniture, with the reds drawn to an imposing desk, but expert Roo Irvine isn’t convinced. Over with the blues, a recent holiday to Kenya and Tanzania inspires Helen to consider a reclining stone hippo sculpture, but Caroline Hawley won’t let them wallow in hesitation for long. In Derby, James Lewis is on the rostrum, and there are hopes and regrets over purchases. Which team will come out on top?

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thrilling new railway adventure following the Silk Road through the most populous country of Central Asia, Uzbekistan. At the meeting point of east and west, this region has been home to some of the world’s greatest empires: ruled by the Mongols, the ferocious conqueror Tamerlane, imperial Russia and the Soviet Union. Michael begins in the capital, Tashkent, where he takes in its vast, vibrant market and samples popular Uzbek delicacies. He travels on the lavishly decorated Soviet-era metro, which was hidden from the outside world until 2018, when a ban on photographing it was lifted. Michael hears how an earthquake resulted in Tashkent becoming the capital of Soviet modernist architecture, and he makes his TikTok debut with a globally famous Uzbek social media star.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In east Yorkshire, police officer Richard Fussey and local farmer Gordon Calvert discover a suspected gang of nighthawkers – illegal metal detectorists accused of stealing historic treasures under the cover of darkness. When the pair stumble upon men digging on farmland near the ancient Roman road of Woldgate, a tense midnight confrontation unfolds. But when back-up arrives, the question remains: will police find enough evidence to prove the crime and protect Britain’s buried heritage? In Hertfordshire, farmers and police fight back against illegal fly tipping on farmland – a problem reaching epidemic proportions, choking the countryside and leaving landowners to foot the bill. And in County Durham, reporter Paul Colgan investigates the enduring crime of livestock rustling, meeting a farmer whose stolen animals cost him thousands, and officers trialling a bold new operation to stop thieves in their tracks.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists head to the beach to try to find a daughter’s lost bracelet, given to her by her loving foster parents. A 160-year-old find leads archive detective Sam on a fascinating trail, which involves the author of Sherlock Holmes, and crafter Sally is inspired by the history of a Brown Betty teapot fished out of the lake.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: See Hear explores how technology is transforming life for deaf people, from communication tools, English-to-BSL translation and AI to new ways of connecting, working and living in a digital world. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined in the studio by Billy-Jack Gerrard, who is passionate about AI and how it is shaping the future. He explains how AI works, how coding underpins everyday technology and how his own experiences as a deaf coder have influenced his AI career path and thinking.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions as four more contenders take the black chair under the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to become the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are the history of the Orkney Islands, the films of Laurel and Hardy, Elizabeth David and Sir Garfield Sobers.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team visit Bristol to help organise a busy and cluttered home. The family, which includes three children under five, is struggling to find space for all their bikes, and the unusual layout of their front room needs some clever thinking to maximise the space and storage.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The team witness a baby boom in the delta. Vianet Djenguet follows lioness Matho as she struggles to raise her cubs alone. Tristen Woodward and Brad Bestelink get a glimpse of Xudum the leopard’s newborn cubs, but dangerous male leopards loom nearby. And with competition growing from the enormous lion pride, Anna Dimitriadis tracks a cheetah mother to the very edge of the territory, where she has been pushed to keep her cubs alive.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel takes the experts across the Atlantic to Ottawa for a special edition set in the National Gallery of Canada. Among the finds are a bronze figure obtained in exchange for a pound of coffee, a desk which started life as a musical instrument, a pair of 19th-century 'whirlygigs', wooden bird scarers, rescued from a chicken coop and worth £10,000 and, the real star of the show, a 16th-century emerald jewelled cross which could be worth £25,000.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Blackburn Cathedral, experts Charles Hanson and Anita Manning are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Charles is valuing a rare Eureka clock and Anita is drawn to four oil paintings of the Ribble Valley. Paul Martin meets up with Michelin-star chef Nigel Haworth to discover how to make the perfect Lancashire hotpot.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway tour through Uzbekistan on Tashkent’s suburban electric railway to one of the most spectacular and secretive pieces of Soviet Cold War engineering. Hidden high in the mountains, Michael discovers an installation straight out of a Bond movie - a solar furnace that concentrates the sun’s rays into a beam so hot it cuts through steel. From Tashkent, he picks up the high-speed train west to Samarkand and discovers how China’s Belt and Road Initiative is re-establishing old Silk Road routes with new railway connections. Arriving in the legendary ancient city fulfils a lifelong ambition for Michael. He is overwhelmed by the magnificent architecture of the Registan and moved by traditional music played at its heart. Finally, at one of Central Asia’s most important archaeological sites, Afrosiyob, 7th-century Sogdian wall paintings reveal the diverse and cosmopolitan history of this former Silk Road capital.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: If you know where to look, Provence is world class when it comes to excellent, affordable produce. Marcus is taking a deep dive into how the French produce and cook two of his favourite ingredients, beef and tomatoes.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers head to Somerset to transform sailors' mess decks at Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. They must turn them into vibrant morale-boosting spaces where the young recruits can cook, eat and socialise. Olympic champion and ex-service woman Dame Kelly Holmes joins Michelle to judge the rooms.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a look at veggies with Jason Manford, Ahir Shah, Holly Walsh and Alan Davies.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Meticulous and methodical, Big John engineers his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it’s flawless. But as the deadline approaches, nothing goes according to plan, and two battles unfold simultaneously: a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable. Precision planning meets a chaotic reality, and one miscalculation on either side could mean catastrophe.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Growing Up Jewish
programme.desc: Four young people on the cusp of adulthood prepare for one of the biggest nights of their lives – their Bar and Bat Mitzvah – balancing culture, religion and the chance to party.

programme.title: The Theory of Everything
programme.desc: A look at the relationship between the famous physicist Stephen Hawking and his wife.

programme.title: Aadmi Aur Insaan
programme.desc: Jai and Munish's friendship is ruined when both of them fall in love with the same girl, Meena. Things take a turn when Munish finds out Jai was involved in the case of a faulty railway bridge.

programme.title: Karm
programme.desc: Asha and Arvind are in love but are not allowed to marry by her astrologer father. They start living together and plan to start a family without getting married, but fate intervenes.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: Mr. Oswal a rich, influential, and domineering man lives with his wife and two sons, namely Jayant and Ajay. He convinces his elder son, Jayant, that he cannot marry the girl he loves.

programme.title: Kal Ki Awaz
programme.desc: When a bank chairman's son is abducted and held for ransom, DSP Ali intervenes but cannot save the child. Later, his world is turned upside down when his daughter is kidnapped.

programme.title: Mr. X
programme.desc: Corrupt officials leave Officer Raghuram to die after he completes a secret operation. He consumes a healing solution with a side effect that makes him invisible and helps him take his revenge.

programme.title: Dharam Sankat
programme.desc: After his mother is killed by Jagira, Gopal is raised by Durga. Years later, Gopal's love for Durga and Birju turns into hatred when he learns they hail from a family of robbers.

programme.title: Numbri Aadmi
programme.desc: Shankar, a noble thief, attempts to kill Rana, his father's murderer, in a bid to seek revenge. However, Rana has powerful friends who stand in Shankar's way.

programme.title: Adalat
programme.desc: A farmer is aving 3 city boys from a tiger's theeth. In return they invite him to the city, where he find out that one of them rape his daughter.

programme.title: Mazdoor
programme.desc: A laborer-turned-businessman faces challenges after the marriage of his children.

programme.title: Dil Tera Aashiq
programme.desc: Thakur Ranvir Singh Chaudhary cuts off his ties with his sister after she marries a man below their status, but after her death the responsibility of looking after her children falls on him.

programme.title: Babu
programme.desc: Babu, a young man, falls in love with Kammo. When a thug rapes her, he murders him out of revenge, which leads to his arrest. After his release, he starts looking after Kammo and her child, Pinky. However, Pinky grows up to be a snob.

programme.desc: Raja, a fisherman is secretly in love with Mona. When wealthy Ravi comes to live with his grandmother, Kamladevi, he sees Mona and falls in love with her.

programme.desc: Roshni, a wealthy heiress, wants to marry Suraj, a poor man, but her mother disapproves as his father is an ex-prisoner. Meanwhile, a series of murders take place and the suspicion falls on Suraj.

programme.desc: Kajal and Anand are two lovers who are separated when Kajal's family refuses to accept their relationship. Years later, fate brings them face-to-face with each other.

programme.desc: Justice Chaudhury is forced to go against his principles when criminal Shanker Singh's brother Jai Singh harasses his speech-impaired daughter Lakshmi.

programme.desc: A waiter, is in love with the famous TV reporter Greeta Kapoor. After a man is murdered, Kapoor shows up at Raju's door to ask him some questions - it turns out that Raju served the dead man his last supper.

programme.title: Chori Chori Chupke Chupke
programme.desc: Raj and Priya, a married couple, hire Madhubala, a prostitute, as the surrogate mother of their child. Trouble brews when Madhubala falls in love with Raj and insists on keeping the child.

programme.title: Kyo Kii... Main Jhuth Nahin Bolta
programme.desc: Raj is a lawyer who finds great success through his habit of lying. However, when his son wishes for his father to stop lying, Raj is magically forced to speak the truth and gets into trouble.

programme.title: Namak
programme.desc: When Gopal, a servant, is announced as the heir to his employer's estate, the siblings of his master's family plot against him.

programme.title: Meet Mere Man Ke
programme.desc: Thakur Jagat Pratap Singh, a man of high principles falls in love with a girl and wants to marry her. However, the difference in their social statuses threatens to maim his plans.

programme.title: Lucky: No Time for Love
programme.desc: The daughters of the Indian Ambassador in Russia, is being molested by a young Russian. She manages to escape and hide in a parked car, belongs to Aditya.

programme.title: Parwana
programme.desc: Parwana, a thief, steals a bag containing explosives, which belongs to a dangerous man. The police then set out to hunt him as he is now labelled a terrorist.

programme.title: Mangal Pandey: The Rising
programme.desc: Mangal Pandey and William Gordon become friends during a war in Afghanistan. But when the East India Company introduces a rifle that uses objectionable cartridges, Mangal Pandey rises in revolt.

programme.desc: A waiter, is in love with the famous TV reporter Greeta Kapoor. After a man is murdered, Kapoor shows up at Raju's door to ask him some questions - it turns out that Raju served the dead man his last supper.

programme.title: Karobaar: The Business of Love
programme.desc: Friends Rajiv and Amar go their separate ways after Seema decides to be with Amar rather than Rajiv. Years later, prosecution lawyer Amar is delighted to see Rajiv in court and accused of murder.

programme.desc: Pooja gets married to Suraj but feels guilty for separating him from Supriya, his former fiancee. When Suraj learns the truth he abandons her, oblivious to the fact that she is pregnant.

programme.title: Numbri Aadmi
programme.desc: Shankar, a noble thief, attempts to kill Rana, his father's murderer, in a bid to seek revenge. However, Rana has powerful friends who stand in Shankar's way.

programme.desc: Rocky, a well-known singer and performer at an elite hotel, becomes the prime suspect when Maheema, a news reporter who was deeply in love with him, is found dead.

programme.title: Baabul
programme.desc: Will Malvika the widower be able to remove her husband Avinash from her heart and mind and marry someone else?

programme.title: Aa Gale Lag Jaa 1994
programme.desc: Roshni, a wealthy heiress, wants to marry Suraj, a poor man, but her mother disapproves as his father is an ex-prisoner. Meanwhile, a series of murders take place and the suspicion falls on Suraj.

programme.title: Gumnaam: The Mystery
programme.desc: Ria, an aspiring actress, lands a lead role in a film and must relocate to Shimla. When she is trapped and kept in a bungalow without any outside contact, her lover decides to find and help her.

programme.title: Mere Jeevan Saathi
programme.desc: An Indian pop star is coming to USA in order to sign a contract with one of the leading music studios. He finds himself heaving one night affair with the CEO of the company. Things go wrong when he find her in his flight back home.

programme.title: Krishna Cottage
programme.desc: A group of college students are forced to take shelter in the mysterious Krishna Cottage for the night. Little do they know that a spirit haunts the cottage.

programme.title: Koi Aap Sa
programme.desc: Simran helps her best friend, Rohan, win over the girl he likes, Preeti. However, trouble ensues when Simran gets pregnant and Rohan is forced to marry her.

programme.title: Mere Jeevan Saathi
programme.desc: An Indian pop star is coming to USA in order to sign a contract with one of the leading music studios. He finds himself heaving one night affair with the CEO of the company. Things go wrong when he find her in his flight back home.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: When two different pieces of flat-pack furniture are delivered to the Farmer and Bitzer, confusion ensues as they start to mix up the pieces. Shaun and the flock are forced to give them a lesson in following instructions.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When secret information is leaked from HQ, all eyes fall on Colonel K. Is it possible he is a traitor, or is there something suspicious about his moustache?

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis needs to pull off a magnificent stunt to save the skate park from closure, so Gran gives him the skateboard of the legendary Kendo 360 to help. When the special board goes missing at the worst possible moment, Dennis needs to dig deep and realise that it's the skater and not the board that's important.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Girl enters a cosplay costume contest to win a trophy and meet her favourite TV star, but needs somebody to wear the back end of her enormous costume. Boy is horrified at dressing as the character’s bum, but he doesn't want to let his sister down. Can he put aside his embarrassment to help Girl win the competition?

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob tries to keep Fred's leg out of harm's way.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers a special lamp that can compact organic elements.

programme.title: Sketchy Comedy
programme.desc: A bunch of bananas, a door handle, a fridge and a tortilla are among the items get the Sketchy treatment!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog get a job in a restaurant kitchen, Frog accidentally makes the soup incredibly spicy. He wants to serve it to a celebrity chef, but Duck wants to stop him!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is a tour guide at a grand stately home, and Frog thinks it's so nice that he decides to move in. When he gets annoyed by the tourists, Frog dresses up as a ghost to scare them off.

programme.title: Counterfeit Cat
programme.desc: An intergalactic emergency has Max averting catastrophe by playing dress-up.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum sends his Sharky mate to Joe and Adam’s house with a request to paint his portrait. But can the boys do it wearing glasses that make everything topsy turvy?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Shini and Abby go access-all-areas with Aaron, Tyler and Cam from One Zoo Three as they learn about life at the zoo. Shini meets the capybaras and joins vets caring for the elephants, while Abby is on night patrol and in charge of bedtime for a baby rhino.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: There’s heart-pumping action in Ouchology, and Dr Xand enters a giant blister to help explain why we get them! And in Bodytricks, we learn a cool trick that will amaze your friends.

programme.title: Girl Troop vs Aliens
programme.desc: The Brighthawks hide Ginny’s family as Orbit search camp, friendships are tested, and the camp is locked down. Meanwhile, Ruby and Oz discover the spaceship has been tampered with.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Joy orders Danny and Mick to clean all the windows outside the leisure centre. At first it is a fun job, but soon they realise that they need to bring in some bigger machinery to reach the higher windows. But do they know how to use it, and what could possibly go wrong?

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Through an accident at the leisure centre, Danny and Mick discover what they think is a treasure map. The hunt is on to follow the golden route, but they end up digging their way into trouble!

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe finally find out which bro is funnier as they take to the stage and perform their best comedy acts to a huge crowd of kids.

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: The dancers are split into pairs to determine the contemporary routine, leading to new tensions on A-troupe.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In this special episode of Deadly 60, Steve Backshall takes a deep dive into the fate of one of the most extraordinary, and endangered, ecosystems on our planet: coral reefs. They may cover less than one per cent of the ocean floor, but they support roughly a quarter of all marine life and are irreplaceable - yet they are in serious trouble. Steve travels to some of the world’s most celebrated reef systems, from the shallows of the Java Sea to the tropical waters of the Maldives. Along the way, he meets all the weird and wonderful creatures that call the reef home, including some of the best-camouflaged creatures and some impressive apex predators - he even gets a telling-off from a clownfish!

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A special episode about King Henry VIII, starring Rowan Atkinson. We meet the young Henry as he struggles with his boring dad Henry VII, before becoming king himself and going through wives like most people go through toothbrushes! Meanwhile, across the world, we meet the great Ottoman leader Suleiman the Magnificent in Turkey and the mad, bad and very dangerous-to-know Zhengde Emperor in China. With, of course, our host Rattus to guide the way!

It’s a chance for the shark saviours to relax, see the sights and play a quick game of frisbee on the beach before everyone reunites for the next phase of their training.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: To investigate why tummy rumbles are so loud when you’re hungry, the doctors call in the supersized stomach of Mega Xand! When Dr Chris and Dr Xand count down the Top 5 Bizarre-est Body Parts You Never Knew You Had, you will discover that the mysterious palmaris longus is so bizarre that you might not have it at all! The big question in Ouchology is whether it will be Dr Chris, Dr Xand or Dr Ronx who gets snotted by their kid quizmasters. If the doctors can put all the organs in the right place on their own bodies, they could win enough points to avoid the snot - but someone has to come last! Elsewhere, for Investigation Ouch!, Dr Chris finds out why real doctors are doing fake operations on fake organs inside fake patients in Nottingham. At the emergency department in Edinburgh, Alice has hurt her head and arm by accidentally running into a fence. And when Finlay arrives with burns on his forearms, the medics think it might have something to do with parsnips!

programme.desc: Dr Chris and Dr Xand continue to find out more from the team of experts and explorers in Greenland. In this episode, they leave their camp high above Store Glacier and head out across the high Arctic seas in the good ship Neptune. They are in search of the amazing sight that is the Petermann iceberg. At a gigantic two billion tonnes of ice, it is the biggest object afloat in the northern ocean. They attempt the difficult and dangerous task of docking the ship to the berg. The ship's captain Ragi is under pressure, as his target is moving.

programme.title: Art Ninja

programme.title: Deadly Mission Shark
programme.desc: Bad weather causes sea sickness amongst the team, and their next diving lesson has to be abandoned. Steve worries they are running out of time and gives his recruits a pep talk to try and boost spirits. He takes the team to Honeymoon Harbour, an island location where they can meet some magnificent rays. Then it's time for a marathon clean-up as half the team comb the beach for litter. Meanwhile, the others join Steve for an underwater clean-up of the seagrass beds that help make the area a shark sanctuary.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: What would it look like if a lifetime’s tears fell in one massive drop? That’s what Dr Chris and Dr Xand are determined to find out in Don’t Try This at Home! Gladiator Dynamite is back to put Dr Dan and Dr Raph’s lungs to the test in an unusual Body Battle. In Investigation Ouch, Dr Xand makes a poo donation in the name of medical science, and minds are bent by Dr Chris and Dr Xand, who use pictures by the mysterious street artist Xandsy to trick the eyes and brains of passers-by. At the emergency department in Liverpool, Elinor arrives with a knee injury after falling during a hurdles race at her athletics club, and Naveah has accidentally swallowed a 20 pence piece on a visit to the swimming pool.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Jack is just 13 years old, but he is already one of the best young darts players in the world. As the British youth darts champion, he has his sights set on an even bigger title: world champion. Jack travels to Gibraltar for the World Junior Open, where he will compete both in the singles and as part of the England squad. However, the road to victory won’t be easy, as Jack has previously lost a world final against Luke Littler, who has since gone on to become professional world champion at just 17. Can Jack turn the tables and bring home the gold? We follow Jack as he trains, competes and faces off against his biggest rivals, including his England teammate Archie, currently ranked number one in the world. With fast-paced action, nail-biting matches and the pressure of playing for his country, we follow the highs and lows of Jack’s road to the top.

programme.desc: This time on Shark Bites, Steve Backshall looks at the most graceful fish in our seas. Manta rays may not look like sharks, but they are part of the same family. Steve swims with them off the coast of Hawaii, observing how their wide, flexible, cartilage skeleton enables them to summersault through pockets of plankton, hovering it up. Back in the Shark Lab, Steve reveals the secrets behind how they glide and fly through the water so effortlessly.

programme.desc: Charity Birmingham Tree People - especially dedicated to adding trees to areas with higher levels of deprivation - work with the community to plant six new cherry trees in Handsworth Park.

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Susan takes in the wonders of Worcestershire, The Malvern Hills and the Midlands. From speed records to time travel, she explores history, speed, luxury, canals - and the world's smallest theatre!

programme.desc: We meet a sheepdog handler husband-and-wife team as they enter sheepdog trials. World-renowned glass artist Jennifer Baker creates a new wall sculpture. And at Chester Zoo, an Okapi might be expecting.

programme.desc: As late summer arrives, Reuben and the gang juggle work, adventures and unexpected drama with the installation of underground pipework at a country house and a trip to Ireland for Clive and sons Miles and Sid.

programme.desc: We encounter a man who fell from a scaffold, impaling himself on a flagpole. And a 73-year-old faces life-changing injuries after an accident at home, and an open neck wound needs emergency surgery.
